primary_accounting_issue: Whether foreign exchange risk on intragroup dividend receivable can be designated as hedged item in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 hedge accounting requirements

primary_authority.reason: This paragraph establishes the fundamental requirement that only assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions with a party external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements, directly addressing the intragroup nature of the dividend receivable

This paragraph provides the specific exception for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items that may qualify as hedged items when they result in exposure to FX gains or losses not fully eliminated on consolidation

supporting_authority.reason: This interpretation provides guidance on hedge accounting for net investments in foreign operations and clarifies the treatment of foreign currency risk hedging relationships within groups
IAS 21 governs foreign exchange differences and elimination of intragroup transactions on consolidation, which is referenced in the conditions for the intragroup monetary item exception

peripheral_authority.reason: Insurance contracts with direct participation features are not relevant to the hedge accounting treatment of intragroup dividend receivables
Distributions of non-cash assets to owners addresses dividend recognition and measurement, not hedge accounting for foreign exchange risk

treatment_families.family: General hedge accounting eligibility for intragroup items in consolidated statements
Exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items
Net investment hedge accounting for foreign operations

approaches.label: Hedge accounting excluded for intragroup items in consolidated statements
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 explicitly prohibits hedge accounting for transactions between entities in the same group in consolidated financial statements, requiring hedged items to involve parties external to the reporting entity

approaches.label: Hedge accounting permitted for intragroup foreign currency risk exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 provides a specific exception allowing foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged items when FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 provides guidance on hedging net investments in foreign operations, which represents a distinct top-level accounting model for foreign currency risk exposure at the group level
